default: I will search the showcase and sust_* catalog for that lane.
Let me match your question to water, electricity, or gas efficient products.
overview: Zyanne here — I turn efficient products into a practical sustainability journey, not just a link list.
Three utility lanes, two product columns — I explain how each step saves energy, water, or gas.
water_lane: Water lane — dishwashers, aerators, leaks, and submetering paths.
Commercial kitchens often win here before cookline gas retrofits.
electricity_lane: Electricity lane — refrigeration, lighting, and ETL cooking equipment.
Marketplace etl_* rows carry grants overlay when enriched.
gas_lane: Gas lane — wok, fryer, and cooking retrofit alternatives.
Pair gas savings with monitoring so waste is visible first.
marketplace_explainer: Good question — here is what Greenways Market Place is and how it connects to what I do in this chat.
Happy to explain the marketplace — the shop and this conversation work together, but they are not the same thing.
product_deal_spotlights: Weekly spotlights are on Zara — I cover search across the full efficient-product catalog.
Deals feed rows are curated offers that can make your sustainable choice easier.
eco_journey: Let us map Home, Restaurant, or Office — tell me your region and I will mirror examples from the eco planner.
Starting or mid-journey — I pull tips from others who already cut energy, water, and waste.
citizen_benefits: Sustainable choices pay back in bills, comfort, and resilience — not only CO₂.
The citizen benefits guide covers wins people often overlook.
recycling: Recycling old equipment can raise your profile and sometimes return cash.
Circular paths are a sustainability story worth telling stakeholders.
product_transition: The field is shifting fast — I will frame what that means for your next product pick.
Transition stories help teams see why efficient equipment is not optional anymore.
low_energy_examples: Here are worked examples of lower-energy products — good for introducing upgrades.
Energy-efficiency stories land better with concrete product comparisons.
european_buildings: European case studies show real benefits after sustainable products and materials.
Buildings and eco materials pages are strong for stakeholder conversations.
product_credentials: ETL-style credentials mean verified efficiency — not marketing alone.
Deep dive pages help you read specs before you commit capex.
sustainability_news: I read sustainability news as context for your upgrade timing.
Headlines matter when they change grants, tariffs, or product availability.
product_grants: I flag products to Andrieus for scheme detail — we can keep searching here.
Grants on etl_* rows need Andrieus to confirm eligibility in your region.
role_resources: These finders and journey guides shape how I advise on Greenways.
Open the full finder when you need every marketplace row, not just chat samples.

deals: Limited-time spotlights live with **Zara** — I search the full catalog lanes.
Weekly feed offers are **Zara**; sustained product search stays here.
equipment: Lifecycle and ETL fit go to **Artemis** after you shortlist products.
**Artemis** owns deep dive, trajectory, and renovation pairing.
grants: Scheme detail on marketplace grants — **Andrieus** confirms eligibility.
I can flag products to **Andrieus** while we keep answering here.
finance: Payback and finance stacks — **Vincent** after you pick efficient equipment.
**Vincent** models unit costs against your upgrade shortlist.
